caption:
This is why it works:

Your phone hits your brain with dopamine every few seconds. After hours of scrolling, your stimulation threshold goes way up. So when you sit down to actually work, your brain rejects it. It's too slow, too boring, not enough.

Staring at a wall fixes that. Boredom brings your baseline back down. After 10 minutes of nothing, work actually feels engaging again.

But that's only part of it.

Your focus is a limited resource and most breaks don't restore it. Checking your phone, watching a video, scrolling a bit... that's not rest. That's more input. Your brain never gets a chance to recover.

Doing nothing activates your Default Mode Network, which is basically your brain's recovery mode. It's what actually restores your ability to concentrate.

10 minutes of nothing. Then 2 hours of everything.

The deep work isn't the hard part. Surviving the wall is.

I built Eclipta to train exactly this. Download it from the link in bio.
